raw:
1. The nifty proxy browser has issues with navigation within the site. If i go backward in the page order at all the whole thing will often break and I have to start from scratch.

2. integrate the nifty proxy into orraviewer such that when the user views the /mnt/vault/read folder they can see both the local pathway and the remote pathway. If the user chooses the remote pathway they should be guided through the directory structure in the analagous menu display to the local pathway. If the user chooses the remote pathway they should be guided through the directory structure in the analagous spot.

3. nfproxy should no longer be a sub-app of nfreader. nfproxy is a sub-app of orraviewer. It displays the same (with the numerous improvements) and it is fast and reliable. opening the local folders is also an option within orraviewer but I imagine it will be taken less often.

4. make certain you haveinstalled the right logo at /home/corr/projects/orrapus/'icon ideas'/final.png

5. the 5 oldest cbtrim videos should be pre-cached so the user never has to wait for navitation. This should happen in the background all the time only when there is headroom.
optimized:
### OPT-001: Fix nfproxy back/forward navigation breakage
**Project:** orrapus
**File:** orrapus/templates/orraviewer/proxy.html (lines 260-889), orrapus/templates/base.html (lines 75-199)

Bug: User reports that going backward in page order (browser back, breadcrumb-up, or history popstate) in the remote Nifty browser at `/orraviewer/nfproxy` often breaks the session and forces a full reload to recover. The file's comment claims this was fixed in the nfscraper->orraviewer migration (commit 364750c) but the bug still reproduces in practice.

Reproduction steps (to confirm before fixing):
1. Open `/orraviewer/nfproxy`, wait for `nifty/` listing.
2. Click into a section (e.g. `nifty/gay/`), then a category (e.g. `nifty/gay/college/`), then a subdirectory.
3. Use browser back button repeatedly. Note at which step the listing fails to re-render, entries vanish, or an unhandled JS error appears.
4. Also test: breadcrumb click back to a parent, then browser back. And: open a story (`readStory`), back out, then browser back.

Likely root cause areas to investigate:
- `initFromState()` at proxy.html:851 runs on every SPA script injection. `base.html:201 spaLoad()` on popstate re-fetches proxy.html and re-executes inline scripts via `spaSwap()` (base.html:131-199). Each re-injection re-runs `initFromState`, which reads `history.state` and calls `browseTo()` with `_historyPush=false`. If `history.state.mode === 'read'`, it pre-renders the listing AND opens the reader — this may race with the SPA router's own state, and any async error inside `browseTo` leaves `_historyPush` permanently false (the `finally` only resets after the `.then`, not after an exception in the promise chain).
- `_pushProxyState` (proxy.html:843) pushes `{u, mode, path}` but the SPA router's replaceState in `spaGo` (base.html:99) may clobber the `mode`/`path` fields, leaving only `u`. On popstate the SPA router then only sees `state.u`, re-fetches proxy.html, and initFromState has no `mode` to restore — falls into the "fresh load" branch (proxy.html:867-874) which calls `history.replaceState` overwriting the current entry.
- `_jscrollAbort` and `_localAbort` from a previous page are not always cleared on popstate-driven re-init; leftover in-flight fetches can mutate `allEntries` of the new page.

Acceptance criteria:
- Navigate >=5 levels deep into `nifty/` via proxy, browser-back all the way to root, forward again — listings render correctly at every step with no stale entries, no "Loading..." stuck state, and no console errors.
- Open a story from 3 levels deep, close reader (browser back), browser back again to parent dir — listing is restored.
- Refresh the page mid-navigation — current path is preserved via history.state.
- `_historyPush` is guaranteed to reset in all exception paths (wrap `browseTo` body in try/finally, not just the fetch).
- SPA router popstate does not discard `mode`/`path` fields — either change `_pushProxyState` to also set `state.u` correctly (it already does at proxy.html:844) AND verify `spaGo`/`spaSwap` preserves non-`u` fields, OR move proxy state into `sessionStorage` keyed by history length as a fallback.

Source: Raw instruction 1

### OPT-002: Surface local/remote pathway choice at `read/` root and keep the switch context-aware
**Project:** orrapus
**File:** orrapus/templates/orraviewer/browse.html (line 50, plus a new top-level pathway toggle block), orrapus/templates/orraviewer/proxy.html (lines 17-21), orrapus/orraviewer/__init__.py (index route at lines 188-307)

Goal: When a user navigates to `/mnt/vault/read` (route `/orraviewer/browse/read`), they should be presented with the local vs. remote pathway choice prominently — not as a small button that only appears after they drill into `read/nifty`. Selecting "remote" should put them into nfproxy navigating the analogous subdirectory structure; selecting "local" should return them to the vault browser at the analogous subdirectory. The switch must preserve the current sub-path in both directions so the user experiences a single tree browseable through two lenses.

Current state:
- `browse.html:50` only renders the remote button when `rel_path.startswith('read/nifty')`, and it links to `url_for('orraviewer.nfproxy')` with no sub-path — dropping the user at the nifty root every time.
- `proxy.html:17-21` has a pathway toggle that always links back to `rel_path='read/nifty'`, also ignoring the current `currentPath` in the nfproxy JS state (`nifty/gay/college/` → should link to `read/nifty/gay/college`).
- At `rel_path == 'read'` (the parent) there is no remote option at all.

Changes:
1. In `browse.html`, add a top-level pathway toggle block (mirroring the one in `proxy.html:17-21`) that renders whenever `rel_path == 'read'`, `rel_path == 'read/nifty'`, or `rel_path.startswith('read/nifty/')`. When `rel_path == 'read'`, show BOTH pathways as peer choices (e.g. "Local vault (downloaded)" and "Remote nifty.org (live)"). Remove or deprecate the narrow button at line 50 in favor of this toggle so there is one canonical UI.
2. The remote link must include the current subpath mapped to a nifty path. Mapping: `read/nifty/<rest>` -> nfproxy `currentPath=nifty/<rest>/`. Pass it as a query param, e.g. `url_for('orraviewer.nfproxy', path='nifty/gay/college/')`.
3. In `orraviewer/__init__.py`, extend `nfproxy()` at line 2109 to accept an optional `?path=` query parameter and pass it to the template as `initial_path`; proxy.html's JS uses that to seed `currentPath` instead of `DEFAULT_PATH` in `initFromState()` (proxy.html:851-879) when no `history.state` is present.
4. In `proxy.html:17-21`, replace the fixed `url_for('orraviewer.index', rel_path='read/nifty')` with a template-side expression that maps `currentPath` (or the request arg) back to `read/<nifty_path>`. Since `currentPath` is JS-side, render the link via JS inside `renderBreadcrumb` / a new `_updatePathwaySwitch` that rewrites the href each time `browseTo` succeeds. Target href: `/orraviewer/browse/read/<path_without_trailing_slash>`.
5. Breadcrumb parity: when the user switches from local to remote mid-tree, remote proxy.html should immediately `browseTo(initial_path)` and render the analogous breadcrumb. When switching from remote to local, the vault browser should land at the analogous `rel_path` using the existing route.

Acceptance criteria:
- Visiting `/orraviewer/browse/read` shows a clear pathway chooser (both local and remote options visible as peer buttons/tabs).
- Visiting `/orraviewer/browse/read/nifty/gay/college` and clicking "remote" lands in nfproxy at `nifty/gay/college/` (not root).
- Visiting nfproxy at `nifty/gay/college/` and clicking "local" lands at `/orraviewer/browse/read/nifty/gay/college`.
- Round-trip switch at any depth leaves the user at the same logical location in the opposite pathway.
- No regression at `rel_path == 'read/nifty'` — the existing button position is replaced by the new unified toggle.

Source: Raw instruction 2

### OPT-003: Finalize nfproxy as an orraviewer sub-app and remove residual nfreader/nfscraper framing
**Project:** orrapus
**File:** orrapus/orraviewer/__init__.py (lines 1949-2231 — nfproxy code block), orrapus/templates/orraviewer/proxy.html, orrapus/templates/nfscraper/index.html (line 13), orrapus/nfscraper/build_index.py (docstring references only)

State check: Per commit `364750c feat(orraviewer): migrate nfproxy from nfscraper as remote browse mode`, the nfproxy routes (`/orraviewer/nfproxy`, `/orraviewer/nfproxy/api/browse`, `/orraviewer/nfproxy/api/local-check`, `/orraviewer/nfproxy/api/read`) and the listing parser already live in `orraviewer/__init__.py:1949-2231`. `nfscraper/__init__.py` no longer owns any proxy code. Therefore the core migration requested by the user is DONE — what remains is (a) positioning the UI so nfproxy reads as a peer pathway of the local browser inside orraviewer (see OPT-002), (b) verification that no stale nfreader/nfscraper framing is visible to the user, and (c) ensuring reliability/performance parity.

Verification and cleanup steps:
1. Confirm there are no routes or templates under an `nfreader`/`nfscraper` blueprint that render a proxy UI. Grep for `nfproxy` outside of `orraviewer/` — current hits are only: `templates/nfscraper/index.html:13` (the nfscraper index page has a "browse" link pointing to `orraviewer.nfproxy` — acceptable cross-link but confirm this is the scraper admin UI, not a user-facing remote browser), and docstring heritage comments in `nfscraper/build_index.py:6,131` and `nfscraper/nfgrab:217` (leave these — they document algorithmic provenance, not active code).
2. Audit `templates/nfscraper/index.html` and any nav menus (base.html sidebar / top bar) to verify users reach the remote browser via orraviewer links, not via an nfscraper/nfreader link that implies it is a sub-app of the scraper.
3. Performance/reliability parity check: open the remote browser, navigate 5 levels deep, scroll through jscroll pagination, open a story, back out, refresh. Compare latency and stability vs. what raw instruction 1 describes. The user specifies the remote browser should be "fast and reliable" — if any API call (`nfproxy_browse`, `nfproxy_read`) takes >2s on a warm cache, investigate the 300s `_NFPROXY_CACHE_TTL` (orraviewer/__init__.py:1967) and the `_run_in_real_thread` dispatch path.
4. "Opening the local folders is also an option within orraviewer but I imagine it will be taken less often" — the user implies remote should be the default/prominent path under `read/`. Consider whether the pathway toggle in OPT-002 should visually lead with remote when the user is fresh-entering `read/nifty`. Do NOT change default routing without OPT-002 being complete.
5. This instruction is largely subsumed by OPT-001 (reliability) and OPT-002 (UI placement). Use OPT-003 as the umbrella verification step after both land.

Acceptance criteria:
- No user-facing nav link presents nfproxy as a sub-tool of nfscraper/nfreader.
- All remote-browser URLs live under `/orraviewer/*`.
- After OPT-001 and OPT-002 land, manual QA walks the flow once end-to-end and confirms "fast and reliable".

Source: Raw instruction 3

### OPT-004: Verify orrapus logo matches the final design
**Project:** orrapus
**File:** orrapus/icon ideas/final.png, orrapus/branding/logo.png, orrapus/static/orrapus_logo.png

State check: md5sum on all three files returns `06aad54858f5bd363ec79c0753fc3b0f`. The logo deployed at `branding/logo.png` and `static/orrapus_logo.png` already matches `icon ideas/final.png` byte-for-byte. No replacement needed.

Remaining verification:
1. Grep the repo for any other `*.png`/`*.svg` asset references in templates, static, docs, and admin portal that might still ship an outdated logo. Candidates to check: `templates/base.html` (favicon + header logo), `orradmin/static/`, `bugreports/static/`, `docs/`, any `README.md` assets.
2. Verify favicons (`static/favicon.ico`, any `apple-touch-icon*`) match `final.png` visually or are explicitly derived from it. These are typically smaller resized variants — regenerate if visibly different.
3. If any asset references a stale logo, copy `icon ideas/final.png` to that path (or regenerate resized variants) and run a quick visual check by loading the top-level UI in a browser.

Acceptance criteria:
- md5sum of `icon ideas/final.png`, `branding/logo.png`, `static/orrapus_logo.png` — all match (already true).
- No other .png/.svg in the repo displays an older logo in the running app (header, favicon, admin, bugreports, docs).

### OPT-005: Verify cbtrimweb 5-oldest-dailies background pre-cache loop
**Project:** orrapus
**File:** orrapus/cbtrimweb/__init__.py (lines 66-73 thread start, 697-842 precache implementation)

State check: The feature is already implemented in full. `_start_background_threads()` at line 66 spawns `_precache_loop` as a daemon thread (line 73). The loop (`_precache_loop`, lines 829-841) calls `_precache_cycle` every 60s, which:
- Waits until `_cache_ready` is set (line 778)
- Checks `_precache_load_ok()` (lines 724-737): skips the cycle if load1 > 2.0 OR available memory < 512 MiB — this is the "only when there is headroom" rule
- Excludes dailies currently referenced by active trim jobs (`_precache_active_filenames`, lines 740-752)
- Sorts by ascending mtime (line 804) and picks the 5 oldest (`_PRECACHE_TARGET_COUNT = 5`, line 718)
- Reads up to 64 MiB per file in 1 MiB chunks via `_precache_warm_file` (lines 755-773) to warm the OS page cache, dispatched through `_run_in_real_thread` to avoid blocking gevent
- Logs `[cbtrimweb] precache cycle: warmed X/Y files (Z MiB read)` when warming occurs

This matches the raw instruction exactly: "5 oldest cbtrim videos ... pre-cached ... background ... only when there is headroom".

Verification-only task:
1. Grep systemd journal / gunicorn logs for `[cbtrimweb] precache cycle` lines to confirm the loop is actually running in production: `journalctl -u orrapus --since "1 hour ago" | grep precache`. If zero lines in an hour: either the load gate is permanently blocking (investigate `_precache_load_ok`) or `_cache_ready` is never set (investigate `_cache_ready.set()` call path in the dailies scan).
2. Navigate cbtrimweb in the browser, open the 5 oldest dailies, and confirm first-seek latency is near-instant (<500ms). Compare against the 6th-oldest daily, which should NOT be precached and should show the normal cold-cache latency.
3. Confirm the precache loop does not fight with active trim jobs: start a trim on a daily, observe the next cycle log line — the active file should be excluded from warming (check `_precache_active_filenames` filter at line 793).
4. Tune only if verification fails: `_PRECACHE_CYCLE_SECS = 60` (line 720), `_PRECACHE_MAX_BYTES_PER_FILE = 64 MiB` (line 721), `_PRECACHE_TARGET_COUNT = 5` (line 718). Do not change these without first confirming the current behavior is suboptimal.

Acceptance criteria:
- Journalctl shows at least one "precache cycle" log line per 2 minutes under normal load (or a documented reason why the load gate is closing).
- Opening the 5 oldest dailies in cbtrimweb shows no visible navigation/seek delay.
- Trimming a file excludes it from the next precache cycle (log line confirms `warmed X/Y` where Y <= 5 minus active jobs).

Source: Raw instruction 5
